feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series: Legos, Motorbikes and Vintage Cars
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

My name is Pierre, and I'm 62 yo.

I worked as a Senior Director, Product Communications at SAP, first for SAP Intelligent RPA as Head of Communications then for SAP Build as the oldest member, by far 😉, of the Product Communications and Enablement Team, with wonderful colleagues that I miss. 🩵

Since April 2025, I'm not actively working for SAP anymore, as I was lucky enough to be eligible for the Voluntary Early Retiring Plan launched by SAP in 2024. On one side I really miss the wonderful colleagues I was working with, but on the other side I'm very happy to have more time for my hobbies.

So I’d like to share a bit about my hobbies as a part of the  SAP Community Hobbies series.

 

First hobby: building Legos

I have always loved building things with Lego: my brother Jean-François and I got our first Lego set when I was 5, and throughout our childhood we played Lego a lot, with our father guiding us. We built houses, castles, and also vehicles with the electric motor etc.

Then in 1978, at age 15, I discovered the first microcomputers, and I turned away a bit from Lego to code my first computer programs.
You will find more about my beginnings as a progammer in the blog post Blog it Forward – Pierre Col I published exactly 6 years ago.

It's also because I love building from scratch that I was very happy to work for SAP Build: this low-code + pro-code solution allows business users and developers to very easily build applications, automated processes, and websites.

For Christmas 2024, my youngest daughter offered me the Lego Porsche 911 set: 1458 parts, to build either a Turbo or a Targa. I have completed the building in a few months:

I like this activity very much, because building Legos really helps me relaxing.
To be continued!

 

Second hobby: riding my vintage bikes

You will understand that I'm a petrolhead. 😉

First, I have always been into motorbikes, because my father was a biker.

From Norton...

In 1973, when I was 10 yo, he sold the Norton 88 I've ever seen in the garage to buy a magnificent  '1970 Norton Commando.

And every morning he was driving me to the high school, on the way to his work. You can easily imagine how proud I was...

My father always kept this bike in his garage, riding it until he was 75 yo. When I was visiting my parents, I regularly did some small tours on the Norton to keep it up and running.

When my father passed, almost 4 years ago, in accordance with my brother I inherited his bike. I did not ride it since then, and I will spend some time this Spring to restart it and ride it again in the countryside around Lyon...

You can hear the wonderful sound of the Norton Twin roaring here:

... to Honda...

In 1981, my first motorbike was a Honda 750 Four. With 67 hp for 220 kg, it was for me an incredibly powerful bike. In 1987 I bought a Honda CB 900 Bol d'Or which I still own:

I lent it in 1997 to an uncle living in Grenoble, he stills ride it but as he is 75yo now I imagine that he won't ride it for very long and I should get the 900 Bol d'Or back in my garage in a couple of years.

... and finally to Ducati

Since 1997, I ride Ducati motorbikes. It started with a 916, then I had a 350 Mark3 Desmo, a 900 Supersport, a 998 S, a Desmosedici RR and a Panigale 1299 R Final Edition. Yes, I really like fast red bike made in Bologna!

The Panigale Final Edition, with 209 hp for 168 kg, was the most powerful and amazing bike I'ev ever ridden. Indeed, even if it was a MotoGP replica with a V4 engine revving up to 14,200 rpm, the Desmosedici RR was not as efficient as the Panigale...

And I really enjoyed the very light and agile '1973 350 Mark3 Desmo I had during a few years, riding it on teh road as on tracks:

Unfortunately, fancy bikes attract thieves: bought in 1997, my 916 was stolen in 1999. My 998 S bought in 2002 and my rare Desmosedici RR #282 bought in 2007 were stolen in 2011. And my Panigale 1299 R Final Edition #322 bought in 2017 was stolen in 2023.

After some bastards stole my Panigale, I refurbished my good old '1994 900 Supersport which I bought in 2000, and lent to a friend from 2004 to 2019. I'm really enjoying riding this light (180 kg) and powerful enough (80 hp) bike on the curvy road in the hills, one hour northwest from Lyon:

 

Third hobby: driving my vintage 911 Porsche cabriolet

In 2011, right after insurance reimbursed my stolen Desmosedici RR, a good friend of mine decided to sell his 1990 Porsche 911 Carrera 2 cabriolet. It was the same car that hank Moody was driving in "Californication", but in better shape, with 135,000 km on the odometer. 😉

We quickly had a deal, and since more than 14 years I enjoy riding this vintage car, in any weather condition. I just passed 180,000 km and it works perfectly fine. According to my mechanic, my Porsche could reach 250,000 km without any trouble, and as I drive less than 5,000 km per year, for sure it will survive me:


As my wife will be very busy in May 2026, I decided that I will do a "5,000 km in 3-4 weeks" road trip across Central Europe in my Porsche 911: I will pay a visit to my former colleagues in Walldorf, Berlin and maybe a few other cities in Germany before heading to Poland, Hungary, Slovakia, Czech Republic, Austria, Switzerland...

 

Feel free to say hello if you would like me to pay you a visit, between May 1st and May 23: I will be more than happy to let you drive my Porsche in your neighborhood! 😊

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series: Writing Between Silence and Experience
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

My name is Hakan. I’m a husband and a father, and my two girls are my greatest inspiration in life. I work as an Expert in SAP HANA Solution Support, and today I’d like to share a bit about my hobby as part of the SAP Community Hobbies series. Alongside the technical world, one constant has stayed with me since my youth: writing. Writing is not a project for me, but a state of mind. It’s the moment when experiences, thoughts, and encounters begin to take shape, whether they come from everyday life or from situations far outside the comfort zone.
 

Taking a moment to enjoy the view over Lake Lünersee after a long glacier climb.

Over the years, writing has become the place where movement turns into reflection, intensity into clarity, and silence into meaning.
 
 
A quiet moment in the Mongolian landscape, letting distance and silence speak.
 

How and when did you start this hobby? What sparked your interest?
I started writing very early, long before my notes turned into books. Even as a teenager, I wanted to capture what life does to me, traveling, people, nature, silence, and also the intense or challenging moments. I lived a fast and intense life: mountains, horses, archery, film and stunt projects, traveling through remote regions, motorcycling, martial arts, and even periods where I spent time forging metal and making knives. 


Forging metal taught me focus, patience, and respect for the process.



These were moments that demanded absolute focus. For a period of time, I even lived as a monk, an experience that taught me the value of silence and observation. All of this leaves traces. Writing became the place where I could sort those impressions and give them meaning.
 
 
What do you love most about your hobby?
Writing connects the outer world with the inner one. It is also a form of relaxation for me, a mental reset. When I stand on a mountain or ride my motorcycle, that’s when the clearest thoughts come. In challenging situations, my mind switches into deep concentration, and those moments often become the source of later inspiration. Writing shows me what a moment truly meant and preserves something that would otherwise fade.
 

In the Alps, moving through terrain that demands focus and trust.
 
 
What challenges have you faced, and how did you overcome them?
The biggest challenge is honesty, not toward others, but toward yourself. Writing confronts you with topics you may not immediately have words for. And then there is perfectionism: the belief that something must be “perfect” before you share it. I learned that authenticity matters more than perfection. Sometimes an unfinished thought touches more deeply than a polished one.
 
 
What has been your proudest moment or most meaningful experience?
Several readers told me that my writing expressed feelings they had carried for years but couldn’t articulate. Some said they had to pause because the words were emotionally overwhelming. Knowing that something I first wrote only for myself could give someone else comfort or clarity is incredibly meaningful to me.
 

Balance, patience, elevation.
 
 
What tools or resources would you recommend to beginners?
A pen. A digital document. A quiet moment. You don’t need more than that. Technology plays only a small role. What matters is interest, openness, and the willingness to reflect. In both writing and coaching, the approach is less about technique and more about mindset.
 
 
What tips would you give someone who wants to start?
Don’t wait until you feel ready. Perfection is the biggest enemy of beginning. Write for yourself, not for others. Curiosity is the driving force behind everything, writing, learning, and being human. And remember: your experiences are your greatest raw material. Take them seriously.
 
 
This is where thoughts settle, after the climb.
 
 
How do you continue learning and developing in your hobby?
I learn by experiencing. I travel, observe, listen, try new things, and actively seek new impressions. Whether in workshops, in nature, in conversations, or on the road, every moment contains the potential to learn something. Writing helps me make sense of those experiences. Coaching complements this because it teaches me to listen more deeply, to perceive more subtly, and to shift perspectives. Together, these practices give me a level of clarity I didn’t have before.
 
 
Are there any misconceptions about your hobby that you’d like to correct?
Many people think writing is a special talent. For me, it is more about attention and courage. And coaching is often mistaken for consulting. Coaching is not about giving answers, it’s about accompanying people so they can find their own. Both require presence, not perfection.
 
 
Where can people learn more about this hobby?
Books, podcasts, and communities are great places to start, but the best learning happens when you begin writing yourself. Five minutes a day can open a door.
 

A moment of trust.
 
 
How do you see the connection between your hobby and the SAP world?
In my work in Support, two things are constant: complexity and humanity. Writing and coaching help me connect both.
Writing sharpens clarity and expression. Coaching sharpens perception and understanding. Together, they improve how I communicate with customers and colleagues, how I assess situations, and how I share knowledge, whether in KBAs, conversations, or team coaching sessions. At the end of the day, behind every technical challenge stands a person with a concern. And that’s exactly where my two worlds meet.
 
Coming up next 
I’m happy to pass the baton to @Sumaiya, the next member of our SAP Community, who will share her hobby of home gardening and what inspires her beyond work.

Looking forward to discovering many more stories in this series.

Best regards,
Hakan

entry.title: Міжародна Online-лекція: Технології та інновації як рушійні сили зростання бізнесу
entry.content:
Стратегія ERP для України


Бізнес уже не може працювати «по-старому». Епоха трансформації вимагає прозорих процесів, швидких рішень і надійної основи для відбудови українських компаній. Паралельно посилюються вимоги до ефективності, управління ризиками, звітності перед банками та партнерами — і без якісних даних це перетворюється на постійну боротьбу з хаосом.​​

Саме про ці практичні виклики йтиметься на міжнародній онлайн-лекції «Переосмислення бізнесу в епоху трансформації», яку проводить Європейська Бізнес Асоціація спільно з SAP Value Advisory. Під час події говоритимемо про те, чому сучасні ERP-системи стають «нервовою системою» компанії: вони об’єднують фінанси, операції, закупівлі, логістику та звітність в єдиній системі й дають керівникам можливість працювати з узгодженими цифрами, а не з розрізненими файлами.​​

Окремий фокус — український контекст. Як ERP допомагають компаніям:​​

  • швидше закривати звітні періоди й готуватися до аудиту;

  • підвищувати прозорість для інвесторів і фондів відбудови;

  • управляти витратами та грошовими потоками в умовах нестабільності;

  • планувати масштабування в інші країни без побудови «з нуля» процесів та обліку.​​

Дата: 16.01.2026 
Час: 11:00
Мова: англійська 

Спікер лекції — Йожек Грушковняк, директор у команді Value Advisory компанії SAP. Він багато років працює з керівниками та власниками бізнесу в Центральній та Східній Європі, допомагаючи їм прораховувати економіку цифрових змін і впроваджувати стратегії, які спираються на реальні дані, а не припущення.​

Якщо ви будуєте стійкий бізнес, орієнтуєтеся на зростання та міжнародні партнерства і хочете навести лад у процесах та даних, приєднуйтесь до лекції й приходьте з конкретними питаннями про ваші системи, показники та наступні кроки.

До вашої уваги запис першого вебінару, а також прес-релізи на тему Пастка кастомізації: чому конкурентна перевага полягає у стандартизованому ERP-рішенні та Будуємо майбутнє України: чому професійна ERP більше не розкіш, а стратегічна необхідність.

Реєструйтеся за посиланням!

entry.title: Call for Speakers – SAP Inside Track NRW
entry.content:
Call for Speakers – SAP Inside Track NRW

After several years without a regional SAP Inside Track in our area, it’s time to bring the community back together. Therefore We are excited to announce SAP Inside Track NRW 2026 — and we’re now opening the Call for Speakers. Some of you may remember the legendary sitPOTT from 7 years ago. Those events showed how strong, curious, and hands‑on the SAP community in NRW can be. We want to build on that spirit: informal, practical, and full of real project experience.

 

What we’re looking for

We welcome contributions from across the SAP universe, including but not limited to:

  • SAP BTP, CAP, UI5, ABAP, Integration Suite
  • Analytics, Data & AI, Security, Cloud Operations
  • Project insights, architecture decisions, lessons learned
  • Demos, prototypes, experiments, or “this is what we tried and how it went” stories

Whether you have spoken at many events or this would be your first time on stage if you have something worth sharing, we’d love to hear from you.

 

Session format

  • Duration: 30–40 minutes or 15-20 Minutes.
  • Format: Presentation, live demo, interactive session — whatever fits your topic
  • Language: English

 

Event details

  • Event: SAP Inside Track NRW
  • Date: 13. Juni 2026
  • Location: On-Site Adesso Dortmund

 

How to submit your session

Please utilize the following Microsoft Form to submit a Topic Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track NRW.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track NRW
entry.content:
We’re bringing a regional SAP Inside Track back to NRW, and we’d love to see you there. After the memorable sitPOTT from soon to be 7 years ago, it’s time to revive that spirit of learning, networking, and hands on exchange. SAP Inside Track NRW will be a day shaped by the community: practical sessions, real project insights, and plenty of opportunities to connect with people who work with SAP technologies every day.

 

What to expect

  • A full day of sessions from community speakers
  • Topics across SAP BTP, ABAP, UI5, Integration, Analytics, Security, and more
  • Space for discussions, questions, and sharing your own experiences
  • A relaxed atmosphere — no suits required
  • Networking with familiar faces and new ones

 

Event details

  • Event: SAP Inside Track NRW
  • Date: June 13, 2026
  • Location: On-Site Adesso Dortmund
  • Registration: Will be opened soon
  • Admission: free

 

Who should join

Whether you’re deep into SAP or just curious about what’s happening in the ecosystem, you’re welcome:

  • Developers
  • Architects
  • Consultants
  • Students
  • Tech enthusiasts
  • Community veterans (including those who still remember sitPott)

 

Why attend

SAP Inside Tracks are built by the community, for the community. They’re not polished marketing events. They’re places where people speak openly about what works, what doesn’t, and what they’ve learned along the way. If you enjoy practical insights more than glossy slides, you’ll feel right at home.

Here’s what makes attending worthwhile:

  • Practical experience instead of theory Speakers talk about what they’ve actually built, broken, fixed, or learned along the way. No slideware, no buzzwords - just real insights you can take back to work the very next day.
  • Learning beyond your own SAP bubble Whether your focus is BTP, ABAP, UI5, Integration, Analytics, or Security, the sessions are designed to widen your view and connect the dots between disciplines.
  • Open exchange with experts and peers Ask questions, challenge ideas, and get honest answers from people who deal with the same problems you do.
  • A relaxed and welcoming setting Inside Tracks are deliberately informal. No suits, no stage personas, no pressure just people who enjoy sharing knowledge and learning from each other.
  • Networking that happens naturally Conversations start over coffee, during breaks, or while someone shows a quick demo on their laptop. Many long standing community connections began exactly like this including at the old sitPOTT
  • Fresh ideas for your own projects Seeing how others approach challenges often sparks new ways of thinking. Many attendees leave with ideas they hadn’t even considered before.
  • Reconnecting the NRW SAP community It’s been a long time since the last event in NRW. This event is about bringing that spirit back and giving the regional SAP community a place to meet, exchange, and grow again.

entry.title: Announcement: SAP Inside Track NRW – Be part of NRW’s biggest SAP event in 2026
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track NRW

We’re bringing a regional SAP Inside Track back to NRW, and we’d love to see you there. After the memorable sitPOTT from soon to be 7 years ago, it’s time to revive that spirit of learning, networking, and hands‑on exchange. SAP Inside Track NRW will be a day shaped by the community: practical sessions, real project insights, and plenty of opportunities to connect with people who work with SAP technologies every day.

 

What to expect

  • A full day of sessions from community speakers
  • Topics across SAP BTP, ABAP, UI5, Integration, Analytics, Security, and more
  • Space for discussions, questions, and sharing your own experiences
  • A relaxed atmosphere — no suits required
  • Networking with familiar faces and new ones

 

Event details

  • Event: SAP Inside Track NRW
  • Date: June 13, 2026
  • Location: On-Site Adesso Dortmund
  • Registration: Will be opened soon
  • Admission: free

 

Who should join

Whether you’re deep into SAP or just curious about what’s happening in the ecosystem, you’re welcome:

  • Developers
  • Architects
  • Consultants
  • Students
  • Tech enthusiasts
  • Community veterans (including those who still remember sitPOTT)

 

Why attend

SAP Inside Tracks are built by the community, for the community. They’re not polished marketing events. They’re places where people speak openly about what works, what doesn’t, and what they’ve learned along the way. If you enjoy practical insights more than glossy slides, you’ll feel right at home.

 

Here’s what makes attending worthwhile:

  • Practical experience instead of theory Speakers talk about what they’ve actually built, broken, fixed, or learned along the way. No slideware, no buzzwords - just real insights you can take back to work the very next day.
  • Learning beyond your own SAP bubble Whether your focus is BTP, ABAP, UI5, Integration, Analytics, or Security, the sessions are designed to widen your view and connect the dots between disciplines.
  • Open exchange with experts and peers Ask questions, challenge ideas, and get honest answers from people who deal with the same problems you do.
  • A relaxed and welcoming setting Inside Tracks are deliberately informal. No suits, no stage personas, no pressure just people who enjoy sharing knowledge and learning from each other.
  • Networking that happens naturally Conversations start over coffee, during breaks, or while someone shows a quick demo on their laptop. Many long‑standing community connections began exactly like this including at the old sitPOTT
  • Fresh ideas for your own projects Seeing how others approach challenges often sparks new ways of thinking. Many attendees leave with ideas they hadn’t even considered before.
  • Reconnecting the NRW SAP community It’s been a long time since the last event in NRW. This event is about bringing that spirit back and giving the regional SAP community a place to meet, exchange, and grow again.

entry.title: Call for Speakers – SAP Inside Track NRW
entry.content:
After several years without a regional SAP Inside Track in our area, it’s time to bring the community back together. Therefore We are excited to announce SAP Inside Track NRW - and we’re now opening the Call for Speakers. Some of you may remember the legendary sitPOTT from 7 years ago. Those events showed how strong, curious, and hands on the SAP community in NRW can be. We want to build on that spirit: informal, practical, and full of real project experience.

 

What we’re looking for

We welcome contributions from across the SAP universe, including but not limited to:

  • SAP BTP, CAP, UI5, ABAP, Integration Suite
  • Analytics, Data & AI, Security, Cloud Operations
  • Project insights, architecture decisions, lessons learned
  • Demos, prototypes, experiments, or “this is what we tried and how it went” stories

Whether you have spoken at many events or this would be your first time on stage if you have something worth sharing, we’d love to hear from you.

 

Session format

  • Duration: 30–40 minutes or 15-20 Minutes.
  • Format: Presentation, live demo, interactive session — whatever fits your topic
  • Language: English

 

Event details

  • Event: SAP Inside Track NRW
  • Date: 13. Juni 2026
  • Location: Adesso Dortmund (On-site)

 

How to submit your session

Please utilize the following Microsoft Form to submit a Topic.

entry.title: Pix Automatic: How Brazil Is Redefining Recurring Payments — and What This Means for SAP
entry.content:
Brazil’s Pix ecosystem continues to evolve at remarkable speed. After transforming instant payments, the Pix Automatic initiative marks a decisive step toward fully digital, API-driven recurring payments, challenging traditional direct debit models and opening new opportunities for ERP and SAP-based environments.

Pix Automatic is not just a new payment option. It represents a shift in how recurring financial processes are designed, authorized, and integrated.

What Is Pix Automatic?

Pix Automatic enables recurring payments through a single customer authorization, executed automatically on predefined dates, using the Pix infrastructure.

In practical terms, it combines:

  • The convenience of direct debit
  • The speed and availability of Pix
  • A modern, API-first architecture

Typical use cases include:

  • Utilities (energy, water, telecom)
  • Subscriptions and memberships
  • Education fees
  • Insurance and recurring services

Once authorized, payments occur automatically, within limits defined by the customer (amount, frequency, validity).

Why Pix Automatic Is a Game Changer

For customers

  • One-time digital authorization
  • Full control (limits, cancellation at any time)
  • No dependence on cards or legacy debit agreements

For companies

  • Reduced churn and late payments
  • Lower operational costs compared to boletos
  • Access to customers without credit cards
  • Real-time settlement and reconciliation

For banks and ERPs

  • API-based orchestration instead of file-based processing
  • Event-driven payment flows
  • Real-time visibility over execution and failures

The Impact on SAP Landscapes

Traditional recurring payment models in SAP often rely on:

  • Batch processing
  • File exchanges
  • Rigid bank-specific configurations

Pix Automatic changes this paradigm. In SAP environments (ECC or S/4HANA), Pix Automatic enables:

  • I-based payment initiation
  • Real-time payment status feedback
  • Immediate posting and reconciliation
  • Reduced dependency on classic payment files and bank formats

This evolution aligns naturally with:

  • Event-driven architectures
  • Clean Core principles
  • Cloud-first integration strategies

From Concept to Reality: Pilot Projects Matter

While the regulatory framework is defined by the Central Bank of Brazil, real innovation happens in pilot projects, where banks, technology partners, and customers test real-life scenarios.

A strong example applied to SAP is the Pix Automatic API pilot being developed by Santander Brazil in partnership with a SAP Consulting Company.

Why this pilot is relevant:

  • Focus on SAP-native integration
  • Direct consumption of Pix Automatic APIs
  • Real-time orchestration between SAP and bank systems
  • No reliance on traditional payment runs or file-based interfaces
  • Designed for scalability across multiple business scenarios

This type of initiative demonstrates that Pix Automatic is not theoretical — it is enterprise-ready and already being shaped for mission-critical ERP processes.

A Strategic Perspective

Pix Automatic is more than a payment feature. It is a signal that:

  • Banking is becoming API-centric
  • ERP systems must be event-aware
  • Finance processes are moving from batch to continuous execution

For SAP customers, this means rethinking:

  • How recurring receivables are designed
  • How bank integration is architected
  • How finance operations can become more automated and resilient

Those who start experimenting now — through pilots and controlled rollouts — will be better positioned as Pix Automatic becomes mainstream.

Final Thoughts

Pix transformed instant payments. Pix Automatic is set to transform recurring payments.

And projects with some banks and SAP Consulting Companies show that the future of finance integration is already being built — API by API, event by event, directly into SAP.

References

  • Central Bank of Brazil — Pix and Pix Automatic regulatory framework
  • Banco Central do Brasil — Open Finance and Pix evolution
  • Santander Brazil — Pix innovation initiatives
  • Makevalue — SAP integration and banking API projects
  • Industry articles on Pix Automatic and recurring payments (2024–2025)

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: 2026 starts with SAP Champions posts, AI tips and survey to give your opinion!
entry.content:
Happy New Year!  

Our Community Voice is back, and we are more than happy to be present for our SAP Community in 2026. We hope our members got to enjoy some relaxing and festive times and that you are feeling rested and prepared to start this year with the right attitude. 2026 is going to be full of amazing moments and opportunities to keep learning, connecting, and growing our community all over the world. 

To start the year right, we collected some of the great highlights that have already been happening in the first two weeks of January. Very insightful blog posts about new features and recommendations for the best use of SAP technologies, a nice opportunity to explore what is new on our platform, and a look at how our SAP Mentors and Champions have been collaborating within the SAP Community, plus all the great events coming this year! 

Let’s dive in! 

Featured Blog Posts 

SAP Integration Suite New Features Released at the End of 2025 

In Q4 of 2025, SAP Integration Suite delivered great new features, such as AI-powered recommendations that simplify troubleshooting and streamlined connectivity with top platforms like Amazon Kinesis and Box. SAP´s Maiara Ellwanger recently posted a blog post covering the details of these innovations, along with information on new dataspace integration packages, data center availability in new locations, event-driven integrations, B2B interchanges in Trading Partner Management, and new features in Process Integration. Make sure to check out her blog post, as it includes all the details you need to boost your operational efficiency. 

Read Maiara Ellwanger Blog Post here 

Synchronizing BTP Accounts into SAP LeanIX 

One of the main pillars of IT landscape expansion is maintaining transparency and governance. It is important not only to know how systems are connected, but also to understand what exists, why it exists, who owns it, and how it fits into the overall enterprise architecture. SAP Champion Punith Oswal, explains in his latest blog post how LeanIX plays a crucial role with Enterprise Architecture Management, considering that LeanIX focuses heavily on data-driven architecture decisions, ensuring that architecture is not just diagrams, but living documentation. If you want to have a full explanation on how the SAP BTP accounts works in LeanIX and the details on the process (including very helpful images, to make your life even easier finding the configurations), this content is a must read.  

Read Punith Oswall Blog Post here 

Tips on How to Start 2026 with SAP´s AI offer 

Generative AI is more omnipresent than ever in daily lives, and according to SAP´s Leo Irudayam the key to successful AI adoption is a useful case, with the right technology and people. In his blog post, he makes AI tools tangible and understandable by sketching an AI-assisted process of vehicle onboarding, showing how it can be automated by leveraging different AI tools offered by SAP within the Business AI portfolio. Leo also explores SAP´s own foundational model RPT-1 and agentic tasks with Joule Studio. He concludes with a reminder that SAP Document AI is a leader in document processing with inbound and outbound channels, and SAP AI Core a powerful AI orchestration service at low costs each. 

Read Leo Irudayam Blog Post here 

Different approach: UI5 Library Use on BTP by Bundling Libraries at Build Time 

SAP Champion Mio Yasutake published a great blog post presenting valuable tools for simplifying UI5 Library use on BTP, considering two different approaches (with and without CDM) and the benefits and limitations of deploying libraries independently. The highlight of her post is an alternative approach: Bundling the Library at Build time. She explains that the main advantage of this approach are the simpler application configuration and the removal of dependencies on resources defined by the library, such as destinations or HTML5 app host service instances. Mio provides a detailed step-by-step guide with images and all the necessary code. We highly recommend checking it out! 

Read here Mio Yasutake Blog Post here 

Community News 

Tell us what you think and win a virtual meet and greet with Josh Bentley 

We want to hear what you think! SAP Community Advocate Mynyna Chau, recently opened a survey (click here to participate), so our Community Team knows your thoughts, needs and preferences. This is our way to tailor our future community initiatives accordingly, but also because we believe that your input makes a difference. The survey takes just a few minutes of your time and can be completed from your smartphone or computer. Once the survey concludes, we will carefully analyze the results and share a report summary with you.  

And as a token of our appreciation, helping us spread the word enters you into a draw to win a virtual meet & greet with Josh Bentley, head of SAP Community! All you need to do is share the survey link / QR-Code in your social media and comment on Mynyna´s Blog Post here with a link to your social media post that shows how you helped spread the word.  

Hobbies Series: Passion for Writing

The holiday season can be a wonderful opportunity to relax and be available for activities that are often left behind during normal routines. These breaks also allow us to discover hobbies that keep our minds engaged with things we love. At the beginning of this month, SAP Community members shared insights into their favorite activities as part of our Hobbies Series. SAP´s Hakan Haslaman wrote about his passion for writing (blog post here), describing it to shape his experiences and thoughts, whether from everyday life or situations far outside his comfort zone. He shared valuable insights into writing and the topics he enjoys most. He concludes by explaining that in his work in Support, two things are constant: complexity and humanity. And writing and coaching helps him to connect both.  

Events 

Reminder of SAP ALM Summit North America 2026 

As we have previously mentioned before, we would like to remind everyone about the upcoming SAP ALM Summit North America 2026. This event is the chance to boost your ALM value by learning from real-life customer experiences. Expand your knowledge through hands-on labs and attending expert-led sessions.  Some of the amazing speakers that will be presenting at the event are Tim Steuer - VP Customer Communications & Relations SAP SE, Sören Ruder- Head of Customer Experience & Solutions (CXS) SAP SE, Joshua Greenbaum- Principal of Enterprise Applications Consulting EAC and Todd Crandall- Head of Customer Services & Delivery SAP North America. Make sure to register for the event here: SAP ALM Summit NA 2026.  

This year Upcoming Events  

Time flies, and we are already in the middle of January 2026. It is time to organize yourself and book all the great events you cannot miss this year. Check out the list below to see the events already scheduled for 2026:  

  • 20 of January: SAP CodeJam - Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub (Bielefeld, Germa...  
  • 21 of January: SAP CodeJam - Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core ( Hyderabad, India )  
  • 22 of January: SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP ( Hyderabad, India )  
  • 23 of January: SAP Stammtisch Wroclaw - January Edition! (Registration is closed)  
  • 29 of January: SAP Build: Advanced Event Mesh - Event based SAP Build Process Automation (Bengaluru, India)  
  • 09 of February: Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub (Pune, India)  
  • 26 of February:  Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub (Gent, Belgium)  
  • 14 of March: SAP Inside Track Joinville 2026 – Brasil  
  • 17 of March: SAP TALK WOMEN CWB 2026- Curitiba, Brasil  
  • 14 - 16 of April: SAP ALM Summit NA 2026- Pennsylvania, United States 
  • 26 of September: SAP Inside Track Porto 2026 - Portugal  
  • 31 of October: SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2026- Brasil

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Melbourne - Full Day 14th Mar 2026 - Call for Speakers
entry.content:
Happy New Year SAP Melbourne Community , hope you all had a great time off and are fully refreshed and ready for an Awesome 2026

We had a great 2025 with around 9 catch up events to learn and meet the SAP Melbourne Community

Would like to reintroduce a full day event on 14th Mar 2026. 

Please get intouch if you would like to present at the upcoming event. Please send your expression of interest to sapsitmelbourne@gmail.com or you can ping me on 0405083265 

Catch you all soon 

Cheers, 

Sai

entry.title: Безкоштовного навчання від SAP — Defense and security
entry.content:
Український Defense Tech зараз один із найдинамічніших сегментів як в Україні, так і в Європі, і це вже підтверджується цифрами, а не лише очікуваннями.​

Інвестиції в європейський Defense Tech

За даними Dealroom.co, глобальної платформи даних та аналітики про стартапи та інновації, у 2025 році оборонні компанії з Європи, включно з Великою Британією, залучили близько 200 млн доларів інвестицій на ранніх етапах — Pre-seed, Seed та Series A.​
Українські стартапи залучають інвестиції переважно саме на цих етапах, формуючи помітну частку європейського ринку раннього Defense Tech.​

Роль України у Defense Tech

У 2025 році понад 50 українських defense tech‑компаній залучили понад 105 млн доларів приватних інвестицій та грантів, що закріпило Україну серед лідерів Європи в ранніх раундах оборонних технологій.​
Кількість угод зросла у кілька разів порівняно з 2022 роком, а середній розмір раунду збільшився з 200–400 тис. доларів до 2,5–5 млн доларів.​

Чому тут важливий SAP

З огляду на активний розвиток Defense‑сегменту в Україні та зростання вимог до прозорості, логістики й управління ресурсами, варто придивитися до актуальних рішень SAP для галузі оборони та безпеки.​
SAP Defense and Security є одним з пріоритетних напрямів розвитку й продажів SAP, оскільки поєднує ERP, логістику, управління персоналом та аналітику під потреби оборонних організацій.​

Що подивитися: рішення та дорожня карта

  • Огляд рішень SAP для оборони та безпеки:
    SAP Defense and Security.​

  • Дорожня карта розвитку рішень SAP для Defense & Security (включно з актуальними інноваціями до Q1 2026):
    SAP Road Map Explorer.​

Практика в Україні: SAP у війську

У 2026 році цифрова система управління логістичним забезпеченням на базі SAP офіційно введена в промислову експлуатацію у Збройних Силах України, про що повідомило Міністерство оборони України.​
Це означає перехід до системного підходу в обліку, плануванні та забезпеченні військ, з фокусом на прозорість даних і швидкість ухвалення рішень. Читайте про це тут.​

Як це поєднати з навчанням SAP Learning

На SAP Learning є відкриті онлайн‑курси для галузі Defense & Security — і вони повністю безкоштовні за умови наявності облікового запису.  Доступ до них за посиланням.​
Це дає можливість як українським defense‑стартапам, так і партнерам та замовникам SAP краще розуміти, як працюють галузеві рішення SAP S/4HANA Defense & Security, логістика, технічне обслуговування та Explosives Management

entry.title: SAP Innovation Talk Series 2026: практичний гід для лідерів змін
entry.content:
Хто формуватиме діловий ландшафт у 2026 році?
Ті, хто не просто впроваджує технології, а вміє працювати з даними, приймати рішення швидко й бачити повну картину бізнесу.

SAP Innovation Talk Series 2026 — це низка онлайн-сесій для тих, хто керує змінами і хоче розуміти, як зробити бізнес гнучкішим і розумнішим.

Протягом серії розбиратимемо реальні кейси й демонструватимемо, як SAP-рішення допомагають працювати ефективніше у ключових напрямах:

  • 02 лютого 2026: Customer Experience — інструменти для кращої взаємодії з клієнтами.​
  • 03 лютого 2026: Spend Management — розумне управління витратами та стійкіші закупівельні стратегії завдяки ШІ та зв’язаним процесам.

  • 03 лютого 2026: Finance — автоматизація і контроль у реальному часі.

  • 04 лютого 2026: Supply chain — прозорість та гнучкість навіть у складних умовах.

  • 05 лютого 2026: Platform, Data & AI — інтеграція, аналітика, підтримка рішень.

  • 12 лютого 2026: HR — як дані допомагають розвивати команди.

Фокус не на промовах, лише корисні демо, тренди та рішення, які можна одразу перевірити на своєму бізнесі.

Почніть 2026 рік із правильного місця — долучайтеся до SAP Innovation Talk Series 2026.
👉 Реєстрація

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration – Getting started with Google Pub/Sub Adapter
entry.content:
Google Pub/Sub is a reliable messaging tool that lets your systems talk to each other securely using 'topics.' It’s great for sharing data in real-time across different cloud platforms. With the Google Pub/Sub adapter in SAP CPI, you can easily send or receive messages, making it much simpler to connect your SAP processes with Google Cloud 

The Google Pub/Sub Adapter is available in both sender and receiver modes to streamline message-based workflows and comes in two variants — REST and gRPC. 

Key Features 

  • Publish single messages (Basic) or multiple messages (Advanced) at once. 
  • Use gRPC for real-time performance or REST for polling at defined intervals. 
  • Google Pub/Sub accepts only Base64-encoded data, and the adapter can handle encoding automatically. 
  • Provides multiple post-processing options: Acknowledge after Successful Processing, Acknowledge Immediately, and Do not Acknowledge.  
  • Supports Authentication via OAuth2 service account authentication and Workload Identity Federation.  

Practical Use Case Walkthrough 
 
In this section, we will demonstrate how to send SalesOrder data to a topic using the Google Pub/Sub Receiver adapter and then read the message again with the Google Pub/Sub Sender adapter, using the REST protocol for messaging. 

Publish a Message using Google PubSub Receiver Adapter 

Prepare Payload in Content Modifier  
 
For the demonstration, we hardcode the SalesOrder data into the message body. In real time, you can dynamically store the payload from the source system. 

Configure the Google Pub/Sub Receiver Adapter 

Connection tab: 

Parameter  

Description  

Address  

Specify the hostname to connect Google Pub/Sub. 

Authentication Type  

Select the method for authentication to Google Pub/Sub. 

  • OAuth2 Service Account 
  • Workload Identity Federation  

OAuth2 Token URL  

Specify the OAuth2 Token URL which identifies as the authorization server for producing a JWT token internally.  

Client Email  

Specify Google Service Account Client Email. 

Private Key Alias  

Specify the Google Service Account Private Key for authentication.  

Scope  

Specify the scope of the connection to Google Pub/Sub service. 

Reuse Connection  

Enable the reuse of connection objects from the internal connection pool which in turn improves the network turnaround time for multiple communications to the same end point. 

Connection Timeout (in ms)  

Specify the maximum waiting time (in milliseconds) for the connection to be established with Google Pub/Sub service.  

Response Timeout (in ms)  

Specify the maximum waiting time (in milliseconds) for a response message to be received with Google Pub/Sub service.   

Publish Message using Google Pub/Sub Adapter 

Now let’s navigate to the Processing tab, select the Entity as Topic, and select the operation as Publish Message for the use case. 

Also, provide the project ID and topic ID created on the Google Pub/Sub server. 

Processing Tab: 

Parameter 

Description 

Entity  

Select the entity for message processing:  

  • Topics 
  • Subscriptions  

Operation  

Select the operation to be performed.  

  • Publish Message
  • List  

Project ID  

Specify the Google Pub/Sub project ID.  

Topic ID  

Specify the Topic ID created on Google Pub/Sub service 

Operation Type  

Select the type of operation  

  • Basic 
  • Advanced  

Encode Data  

Select the checkbox to encode data to Base64 

Ordering key  

Specify the ordering key for this message.   

Message Attributes  

Specify multiple message attributes as key value pairs. 

Once you run the iflow, you can see the message processed successfully. 

In addition, you can see that the Sales Order message was successfully published to the Google Pub/Sub service. 

Read a Message using Google Pub/Sub Sender Adapter 

The Google Pub/Sub Sender Adapter is available in gRPC and REST formats. For this demonstration, we're using the REST variant. 

Processing Tab: 

In the Google Pub/Sub Sender Adapter, provide the Subscription ID and Project ID from which the message will be read on the Google Pub/Sub Server.  

You can also select the post-processing action once the message has been polled from the Google Pub/Sub Server. 

Once the iflow is successfully deployed, we can check it in the monitoring page. In response, we can see the message body. 

Conclusion: 

The Google Pub/Sub adapter in SAP CPI brings integrations to life by allowing real-time, event-driven communication between SAP and Google Cloud. It enables CPI to easily publish and receive messages at scale, making integrations faster, more responsive, and loosely linked. The adapter's cloud-native design enables the creation of modern, durable integrations that can quickly change with business needs. 

Quick Links: 

  • For information about Google Pub/Sub Sender adapter configuration, see  SAP Help | Google Pub/Sub Sender Adapter.  
  • For information about Google Pub/Sub Receiver adapter configuration, see  SAP Help | Google Pub/Sub Receiver Adapter.  
  • For detailed information about the Google Pub/Sub adapter, see Google Pub/Sub Adapter Guide 

The next blog will take this further with an exciting new use case — so stay tuned for more insights and updates!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Paraguay 2025: AXIGMA impulsa el talento local
entry.content:
El pasado 8 de noviembre de 2025, Ciudad del Este fue sede del primer SAP Inside Track en Paraguay, un evento que marcó un hito en la historia de nuestra comunidad tecnológica. Más de 100 profesionales SAP se dieron cita para compartir experiencias, aprender y construir redes profesionales que perdurarán en el tiempo.

La Formación: La Clave del Crecimiento Profesional
En el ecosistema SAP, la capacitación oficial es uno de los activos más valiosos que un profesional puede adquirir. Obtener formación estructurada, con contenido actualizado y respaldada por partners certificados, abre puertas a nuevas oportunidades laborales y permite dominar las tecnologías más demandadas del mercado. Es una inversión en el futuro profesional que marca la diferencia entre tener conocimientos básicos y convertirse en un especialista reconocido.

AXIGMA: Apostando por el Talento Paraguayo
Queremos expresar nuestro profundo agradecimiento a AXIGMA, quien como auspiciante oficial del SAP Inside Track Paraguay 2025, demostró su compromiso con el desarrollo del talento local de una manera excepcional.
AXIGMA puso a disposición de nuestra comunidad 2 cursos completos de capacitación oficial SAP, cada uno con 100 horas de formación intensiva en tecnologías S/4HANA:

  • Formación del Programa SAP MM - Material Management en S/4 HANA
  • Formación del Programa SAP FI - Finanzas en S/4 HANA

Este tipo de formación representa una oportunidad invaluable para profesionales que buscan certificarse en los módulos más solicitados del mercado. No es solo un curso: es una puerta de entrada al siguiente nivel profesional, con contenido oficial, instructores especializados y certificación reconocida internacionalmente.

¡Anunciamos a los Ganadores!
Tras el sorteo realizado durante el evento, nos complace enormemente anunciar a los dos afortunados ganadores que tendrán acceso a esta formación de alto nivel:

🎓 Pablo Gamarra - Ganador de la Capacitación Oficial SAP MM Material Management en S/4HANA


🎓 Agustín Pastrana - Ganador de la Capacitación Oficial SAP FI Finanzas en S/4HANA


¡Felicitaciones a ambos! Les deseamos mucho éxito en su proceso de capacitación. Estamos seguros de que aprovecharán al máximo esta oportunidad y que los conocimientos adquiridos impulsarán sus carreras hacia nuevos horizontes.

¿Quieres Ser el Próximo Ganador?
El próximo año podrías ser tú quien gane premios como estos. Formación de primer nivel, acceso a contenido exclusivo, oportunidades de networking con profesionales destacados y la posibilidad de participar en sorteos con premios que pueden transformar tu carrera profesional.
Mantente conectado con nuestros canales oficiales y únete a la SAP Community Group para no perderte ninguna novedad sobre el próximo evento.

Gracias a Toda la Comunidad
Desde el equipo organizador, queremos agradecer a todos los que hicieron posible este sueño: participantes, speakers, voluntarios y especialmente a nuestros sponsors, que con su apoyo hacen posible que nuestra comunidad siga creciendo.

Esto es solo el comienzo. ¡Nos vemos en el próximo SAP Inside Track Paraguay!

¿Quieres conocer más sobre capacitaciones SAP? Visita www.axigma.com

entry.title: Від ритейлу до енергетики: як український бізнес отримує міжнародне визнання
entry.content:
Українські компанії з різних галузей — енергетики, ритейлу, промисловості та ІТ-консалтингу — здобули перемоги на SAP Quality Awards 2025 у регіоні Центральної та Східної Європи. Проєкти були відзначені за швидкість впровадження новітніх технологій, інноваційність і відчутний бізнес-ефект. Це підтверджує, що український бізнес не лише зберігає операційну стійкість, а й задає стандарти цифрової трансформації на регіональному рівні.

У 2025 році журі SAP Quality Awards відзначило одразу кілька проєктів з України, які продемонстрували різні підходи до трансформації — від cloud-впроваджень і мобільних рішень до HR-та ритейл-інновацій.

Швидкість і cloud-first — Business Evolution

Компанія Business Evolution реалізувала SAP Cloud ERP лише за 4 місяці, використовуючи підхід Fit-to-Standard і стандартний функціонал SAP. Проєкт отримав нагороду Rapid Time to Value.

Результати після запуску:

  • закриття місяця — 4 робочі дні;
  • автоматизація 80% обробки рахунків;
  • зменшення навантаження на фінансову команду до 30%;
  • повна відмова від власної інфраструктури завдяки cloud-моделі.

 Більше інформації за посиланням.

Мобільність і критична інфраструктура — Оператор ГТС України

ТОВ «Оператор ГТС України» отримав нагороду в категорії Business Transformation – Enterprise Solutions, а також спеціальну відзнаку SAP Quality Director Award.

Мобільний застосунок на базі SAP BTP:

  • повністю замінив паперові процеси;
  • працює офлайн;
  • скоротив поїздки між об’єктами та офісами на 40%;
  • зменшив час обробки даних на 40%.

Рішення вже використовують понад 350 фахівців, і проєкт масштабують далі.

Більше інформації за посиланням.

HR як стратегічний інструмент — Метінвест

Група Метінвест була відзначена за трансформацію HR-процесів через масштабування SAP SuccessFactors на підприємстві «Каметсталь». Проєкт здобув перемогу в категорії Business Transformation – Functional Transformation.

Ключові ефекти:

  • у 5 разів скорочено час оцінювання;
  • у 2,5 раза зменшено витрати на ручну обробку;
  • усунено суб’єктивізм в оцінці персоналу;
  • сформовано культуру регулярного зворотного зв’язку.

Більше інформації за посиланням.

Масштаб у ритейлі — NOVUS

Один із найбільших ритейлерів країни — NOVUS — впровадив SAP Merchandising and Inventory Management у 96 магазинах по всій Україні та здобув перемогу в категорії «Business Transformation – Enterprise Solutions»

Результати:

  • робота з даними в режимі реального часу;
  • удвічі швидша генерація замовлень;
  • зменшення втрат продажів через порожні полиці;
  • відмова від додаткових систем і зниження ІТ-витрат.

 Більше інформації за посиланням.

«Перемоги українських компаній на SAP Quality Awards 2025 демонструють, що навіть у складних умовах бізнес в Україні інвестує в ефективність, інновації та довгостроковий розвиток. Ці проєкти стали прикладом того, як технології допомагають не лише оптимізувати процеси, а й підтримувати економічну стабільність країни». — зазначила Ганна Мікулицька, керівна директорка SAP Ukraine.

SAP Quality Awards — це щорічна міжнародна винагорода, яка відзначає найкращі проєкти впровадження SAP за інноваційність, дотримання принципів якості та вимірювану бізнес-цінність. Цього року премія SAP Quality Awards була представлена у трьох основних категоріях: «Business Transformation – Enterprise Solutions», «Business Transformation – Functional Transformation» та «Rapid Time to Value».

Довідка про компанію

У 2025 році компанія відзначала 30 років діяльності на українському ринку. Сьогодні близько 200 українських організацій, зокрема державні установи, а також підприємства нафтогазового, енергетичного, металургійного, фармацевтичного секторів, сфери торгівлі та професійних послуг, використовують рішення SAP. В Україні існує мережа висококваліфікованих партнерів SAP, які надають свої послуги. Компанія активно допомагає українському бізнесу та благодійним фондам, які підтримують постраждалих від війни громадян. Детальніше про розвиток SAP в Україні — на сторінці, присвяченій ювілею компанії. Ознайомитися можна за посиланням. Більше детальної інформації про компанію SAP та її рішення можна отримати на сайті: www.sap.ua.

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series : My Terrace Garden
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

My name is Sumaiya, and I joined SAP in 2015 as a Scholar and since then have worked in different areas and roles.

Today, I’d love to share a little about my hobby as part of the SAP Community Hobbies series 🌱

What began as a simple interest is now a green space with over 300 plants, including fruit plants, vegetables, ornamental plants, succulents, and cactus. It is my space to slow down, reconnect, and find joy beyond screens and schedules.

Aerial view of my terrace garden

 Once an empty wall, now a layered story of vines, foliage, and flowers growing together.

Succulent Mini garden

 

 

How and when did I start this hobby?

During the COVID period, when we were home most of the time, that old familiarity returned. My husband and I started with just a few plants on our terrace—nothing ambitious. But slowly, one plant became many. Caring for them became part of our daily life, bringing calm, purpose, and continuity in an uncertain time.

Looking back, gardening taught me patience long before I recognized it as a lesson. You cannot rush a seedling or force a fruit to ripen—it grows in its own time, as long as you show up consistently.

Started with Few Palm plants and Jasmine

 

What does my garden look like today?

Our home garden includes fruit plants like pomegranate, chickoo, mango, rose apple, sweet lime, star fruit, mulberry, figs, guava and lemon;

vegetable plants such as curry leaves, Drumstick, chillies, beans, tapioca, sweet potato, coriander;

along with ornamental plants, succulents, and cactus.

We may get just 3–4 mangoes in a season, but the joy has never been about the numbers.

Sapodilla, Figs, Green Pepper (Which post drying in sunlight is used as Black Pepper)

 

French Beans, Mangoes, Pomegranate, Rose Apple and Drumstick (Moringa Plant)

The Bright Colors in the green garden

 

What do I love most about it?

The biggest reward is the experience.

Seeing a plant every day, watching it grow slowly, noticing the first bud, and waiting eagerly for it to ripen—it creates a kind of happiness that’s hard to explain. When your own plant finally bears fruit, it feels like a quiet celebration.

I truly enjoy showing my plants to others. Sharing that space gives me deep, immense happiness and a sense of gratitude for small, everyday wins.

Colorful Blooms that makes the day even brighter

 

Challenges and how I deal with them

Gardening isn’t always easy. Some plants don’t survive. Some seasons don’t cooperate. And because our plants are on an open terrace, weeds have been one of the biggest challenges. Most weekends are spent patiently removing them, making sure they don’t overpower the plants we’ve worked so hard to nurture.

Another persistent challenge is mealy bugs—they require constant attention and effort to control. Since we strongly believe in keeping our garden as natural as possible, we avoid chemical solutions. Instead, we rely on organic remedies, using neem oil and turmeric, which may take more time but align with how we want to care for our plants.

Each challenge has taught me to observe more closely, adapt quickly, and accept that not everything is within my control. Every setback becomes a lesson—for the next plant, the next season.

A proud moment

My proud moments are not big milestones. They are simple, quiet ones:

the first fruit on a plant I nurtured from the beginning,

a plant that survived when I truly thought it wouldn’t,

and a harvest that felt earned, not measured.

One plant I will always remember is a snake plant I gifted myself on my birthday during the lockdown. It arrived in a tiny 3–4 inch pot. As it grew, I repotted it into a larger one, and soon I noticed new plants appearing around it. I separated them into individual pots—and the cycle kept repeating. Today, I have over 30–40 snake plants, and I can no longer tell which one was the very first. Somehow, that makes me smile even more.

Propagating from one single snake plant into so many that covered my walking passage, truly this plant amazes me!

 

As our plants grew, they also became homes for life beyond us. We’ve had bulbul birds visit our terrace frequently, building nests among the plants. Over the past two years, they built nests three times and laid eggs. Watching those eggs hatch and seeing tiny baby birds grow was incredibly heartwarming—it felt like our garden had become a safe space not just for plants, but for living beings too.

BulBul Birds looking for a spot to make a nest

 

 

Bulbul bird nest with 2 eggs hatched

 

More recently, we noticed birds slowly discovering our garden. We began placing bird food, and with time, the number of visitors kept increasing. One evening, we heard the most beautiful chorus of birds and stepped out onto the terrace to witness a magical sight—over 20 Munia birds gathered together, calmly enjoying the space. It was a moment of pure joy and gratitude.

Bird Food we placed thinking Bulbul birds will stay in terrace permanently, but something different happened. Looks like this bird food, Munia bird likes more

 

What makes me most proud is seeing this love for plants extend beyond me. My kids often join us while watering the plants, and my 7-year-old daughter especially looks forward to fruit-harvesting days—those moments turn the garden into shared joy and lasting memories.

These experiences remind me that when you show up consistently and care deeply, growth happens—sometimes quietly, sometimes in ways you never expected.

Tools and resources for beginners  and Tips for getting started

If you are completely new to gardening and worried about plants dying, I always recommend starting simple and stress-free.

A great place to begin is with succulents, especially Mother of Millions. Succulents are incredibly forgiving. You can pluck just one leaf, and it will grow into a new plant. With Mother of Millions, it’s even more fascinating—a single leaf can grow into 10–15 new plants. It requires very little care: moist soil and watering on alternate days, or even once every 2–3 days, works just fine.

Plant : Mother Of Millions

 

Another easy and joyful way to start is by regrowing vegetables in water. Take a small portion of used vegetables, place it in a bottle or container with water, and keep it in indirect sunlight. You’ll be surprised at how quickly new growth appears. A small piece of ginger or sweet potato can grow into a healthy plant with fresh leaves. Even a partly used onion placed in water can turn into fresh spring onions—perfect for a quick salad.

In the Kitchen when i see even a small possibility that the vegetables are sprouting, then instead of cooking them, i put in water bottle to help it grow and then repot in some pot and this cycle repeats

 

Many plants don’t even need soil to begin with. Simply place them in a bottle of water and change the water once a week. It’s minimal effort with maximum joy.

Easiest way to grow plans with minimum effort.

 

You don’t need a big space or expensive tools to start gardening. All it really takes is curiosity, patience, and a little care—and the confidence grows along with the plants 🌱

How I keep improving

Over the years, I’ve learned that not every plant needs the same kind of care. Some grow easily with minimal effort, while others demand time, attention, and patience. Understanding this difference has been one of the most valuable lessons gardening has taught me.

With time, confidence grows—and so does curiosity. I’ve become more comfortable trying new plants and new challenges, learning through experience rather than perfection. Each season adds a little more awareness, a little more patience.

Growth doesn’t always need rigid structure. Sometimes, it simply needs time, consistency, and care.

 

The connection to the SAP world

The connection is stronger than it appears.

Gardening is about systems thinking—understanding dependencies like soil, water, sunlight, and timing. You can’t optimize one part and ignore the rest.

In SAP projects, we build solutions that must be sustainable, balanced, and human-centric. Gardening reminds me that real success comes from nurturing systems patiently, not forcing outcomes.

It helps me show up at work more grounded, focused, and calm.

 

 

What’s next? There’s no finish line here—just more learning, more plants, and more quiet joy. I look forward to continuing this journey, one leaf, one flower, and one fruit at a time.

Thank you for reading. If gardening resonates with you—or if you’re curious about how to start, what to grow, or how to nurture plants at home—I’d be happy to connect and have a chat 🌿

And if you have a hobby that helps you slow down, disconnect, or think better, I’d love to hear about it.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: SAP Joule for Developers, Hobbies Series and Upcoming Events!
entry.content:
We’re happy to bring you another edition filled with highlights, stories, and updates from across our global SAP Community.

There is so much happening right now: from innovative blog posts and hands-on technical content to inspiring personal stories and exciting upcoming events around the world!

In this edition, we explore practical resources to support your SAP BW to SAP Datasphere journey, dive into AI-enabled development with ABAP and Joule, discover a beautiful story from our Hobbies Series, and of course, share important community news and events you won’t want to miss.

Let’s dive in! 

Featured Blog Posts 

Support with functionalities from SAP BW system to SAP Datasphere 

With the end of SAP BW approaching, many companies and developers are diving into SAP Datasphere to migrate reports and build new solutions. In this blog post, based on hands-on implementation experience, SAP member Christian Gravesen, focuses on one specific gap: the absence of standard BW exit variables like Current Month and Current Year in Datasphere. He presents a ready-to-install package that recreates these calendar variables, complete with GitHub access and clear installation guidance. This is a practical resource for anyone working on BW migrations or developing analytical models in SAP Datasphere. 

Read Christian Gravesen blog post here 

AI Enabled Application with ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM 

As AI becomes a core part of application development, SAP Joule for Developers introduces AI-driven capabilities directly into the ABAP world. In this blog post, SAP´s  Swathy explains how to move beyond traditional transactional scenarios by using the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) to build AI-enabled applications. Through her example (generating a travel itinerary and budget breakdown using generative AI), she walks through the supported products, architecture, and key implementation steps. The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM is available in SAP BTP ABAP; SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Cloud Private Edition. Additionally, if you want to keep updated about ABAP AI, there is a list of blog posts thread that you can subscribe to. 

Read Swathy blog post here 

SAP Joule Skill in SAP EHSM- Incident Management and more 

Since the beginning of this year, SAP community member Pardhasaradhi started posting great content about his SAP Joule learning and execution journey. One of his recent blog posts about SAP Joule Skill in SAP EHSM-Incident Management, he gives details about low-code and even no-code setup using OData Service. From understanding Joule capability types to configuring, deploying, and testing a custom skill, the blog provides a clear, practical guide for enabling conversational, AI-driven incident reporting in an enterprise environment. And because he is a highly active member on blogging, we highly recommend following his latest posts to keep learning about SAP Joule Skills in Asset Management Space and more! 

Read Pardhasaradhi Reddy Chelikam blog post here 

Automated Predictive Library (APL) Forecast in a Seamless Planning Model 

SAP´s Max Gander and Marc Daniau recently published a very complete guide on how predictive forecasts built with SAP HANA’s Automated Predictive Library (APL) can be integrated into a seamless planning model. They explained that now with SAP Business Data Cloud and seamless planning, data scientists can now leverage HANA’s Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) and Automated Predictive Library (APL) directly on the HANA database of SAP Datasphere and nicely integrate the results into planning processes. In the step-by-step guide, it is possible to visualize the full architecture, from building the forecast directly in HANA, orchestrating it via task chains in SAP Datasphere, and finally consuming the results as a live version in SAP Analytics Cloud. In the end, they recommend more useful content published in SAP Community. This is a definitely must-read. 

Read Max Gander and Marc Daniau blog post here 

Community News 

Don´t forget, we are still waiting for your feedback!  

In the last Community Voice edition, we explained details about our 2026 SAP Community open survey. For more details, please check Mynyna´s Blog Post here, but we would like to remind everyone that there is still time to participate and give your opinion. The survey takes just a few minutes of your time and can be completed from your smartphone or computer. Once the survey concludes, we will carefully analyze the results and share a report summary with you.  If you share the survey link / QR-Code in your social media and comment on the blog post with a link to your post that shows how you helped spread the word, you are automatically participating into a draw to win a virtual meet & greet with Josh Bentley, head of SAP Community! 

Click here to participate on the survey  

Hobbies Series: Terrace Gardening  

SAP’s Sumaiya recently shared her journey into gardening in the SAP Community Hobbies Series. A hobby that began simply and grew into a thriving terrace garden with over 300 plants, including fruits, vegetables, succulents, and ornamental species. She describes how gardening became a way to slow down, reconnect, and find balance beyond screens and schedules. Through her story, Sumaiya reflects on the lessons gardening has taught her, from patience and consistency to embracing challenges like weeds and pests with natural, sustainable solutions. In the end, she concludes with a parallel between gardening and the SAP world, highlighting how gardening reminds us that real success comes from nurturing systems patiently, not forcing outcomes, which helps her show up at work more grounded, focused, and calm. Besides the beautiful pictures she shared of her flowers, we were more than delighted to learn how this hobby has had such an impact on her life. 

Read Sumaiya blog post here 

Events 

Upcoming SAP CodeJam´s  

This year events have officially started, and one of the greatest opportunities to have a hands-on learning experience is definitely through an SAP CodeJam. Around the world, our Developers team gives their best to bring their expertise and share useful tips on various SAP topics. Check bellow the list of the upcoming SAP CodeJam for the next 2 weeks:  

  • Milan, Italy - Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents ... - SAP Community 
  • Sydney, Austrakua -  Joule Studio: Create Skills and Agents to Ext... - SAP Community 
  • Pune, India - Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP... - SAP Community 
  • Heidelberg. Germany - SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Heid... - SAP Community 
  • Pune, India - Build AI services using the CAP and Generativ... - SAP Community 
  • Paris, France - Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents ... - SAP Community 
  • Melbourne, Australia - Joule Studio: Create Skills and Agents to Exte... - SAP Community 
  • Brisbane, Australia - Gen AI Hub & Joule Studio : Create Skills and... - SAP Community 
  • Paris, France - Real-time data for AI agents: Grounding with ... - SAP Community 

SAP Inside Track Joinville 2026 

Are you part of the Joinville community group? Or maybe you live nearby? One of the biggest events of the year is coming up: SAP Inside Track Joinville 2026! You can expect high-level sessions on innovation, technology, and SAP trends, opportunities to exchange experiences with other community experts, and qualified networking with companies and professionals from the region. The event will take place on March 14 at Ágora Tech Park, Joinville. Make sure to secure your spot and get your tickets through sympla. And follow the Joinville Group to have access to all the updates and news! 

Read more details in Juliane Schmitzhaus blog post here

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Belo horizonte - 21 março 2026
entry.content:
🚀 Chegou o tão esperado momento: SAP Inside Track Belo Horizonte 2026!
Os ingressos já estão à venda! 🎟️

O SAP Inside Track é o maior evento independente de tecnologia SAP do Brasil, organizado pela própria comunidade e focado em troca de experiências, networking e muito conteúdo de alto nível sobre o ecossistema SAP.

SAP INSIDE TRACK BELO HORIZONTE 2026
📍 Local: Localiza Labs, Belo Horizonte – MG
📅 Data: 21 de março de 2026
🕗 Horário: 08h00 às 17h00
🏷️ Formato: Evento presencial

O que você vai encontrar por lá:
✅ Palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócio sobre SAP S/4HANA e outras soluções estratégicas
✅Conteúdo atualizado com as principais tendências e inovações do mercado SAP
✅Oportunidade de fazer networking com profissionais experientes e grandes nomes da comunidade
✅Welcome coffee, almoço e happy hour incluídos no valor do ingresso SAP INSIDE TRACK BELO HORIZONTE 2026

Se você trabalha com SAP, tecnologia, inovação ou quer entrar nesse universo, esse evento é pra você.

🔗 Garanta seu ingresso agora:
Comprar Ingresso SITBH2026

Nos vemos no SAP Inside Track BH 2026! 💛

entry.title: Willkommen!
entry.content:
Herzlich willkommen an alle Interessierten aus dem Raum Basel (CH), aus der Region Freiburg (DE) und natürlich auch aus dem schönen Elsass!

Wir möchten einen offenen, ungezwungenen SAP-Stammtisch aufbauen und laden Entwickler, Berater und Nutzer ein, sich regelmässig mit uns zu treffen – abwechselnd in Basel und in Freiburg. Im Mittelpunkt steht der lockere Austausch rund um SAP: miteinander ins Gespräch kommen, Fragen stellen, Erfahrungen teilen, voneinander lernen und sich vernetzen.

Ob ihr etwas essen möchtet oder einfach nur auf ein Getränk vorbeikommt, bleibt ganz euch überlassen. Wichtig ist uns eine entspannte Atmosphäre, in der sich jede und jeder wohlfühlt. Kommt vorbei, bringt gerne weitere Interessierte mit und gestaltet diesen SAP-Stammtisch gemeinsam mit uns!

Alle, die die Idee des offenen Austauschs und der Zusammenarbeit teilen, sind eingeladen, sich einzubringen, Themen vorzuschlagen oder zukünftige Events mitzugestalten.

Grüsse Marcel und Bastian

entry.title: Der erste Stammtisch Basel - Freiburg
entry.content:
Endlich war es soweit, der erste Stammtisch hat in Basel stattgefunden. In gemütlicher Runde haben wir uns über SAP,  ABAP, Fiori und KI ausgetauscht. Uns kennengelernt und beschlossen, dass auf jeden Fall zu wiederholen 🙂

Der nächste Stammtisch wird in Freiburg stattfinden. Einfach diese Gruppe abonnieren und das Event mitbekommen! Details folgen dann. 

Bastian
